Title: Poaceae-specific β-1,3;1,4-D-glucans link jasmonate signalling to OsLecRK1-mediated defence response during rice-brown planthopper interactions

Common_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: Lectin receptor kinase; plasma membrane receptor-like kinase
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Acts as damage-associated molecular pattern signals to activate defence signalling
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Perceives MLG-derived oligosaccharides and mediates downstream defence signalling required for OsCslF6-conferred resistance to BPH and WBPH
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Recombinant OsLecRK1 bound multiple MLG-derived oligosaccharides with micromolar affinity but not cellotetraose. 2. BGTRIB and BGTETB stimulated OsLecRK1 phosphorylation in vitro and increased OsLecRK1 abundance in vivo. 3. Knockout of OsLecRK1/3 abolished the enhanced BPH/WBPH resistance of OsCslF6-overexpressing plants.
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: MST binding assay; in vitro kinase assay; protoplast assay; CRISPR knockout in F6OE background; insect feeding assay
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Studied in protoplasts and knockout plants during pest interaction
Common_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: BPH infestation; WBPH infestation; treatment with BGTRIB and BGTETB
Common_Genes.Interacting_Proteins: OsLecRK3; MLG-derived oligosaccharides BGTRIA; BGTRIB; BGTETB; BGTETC
Common_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: oslecrk1/3 and F6OE4 oslecrk1/3 seedlings were smaller than wild type under normal conditions
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Valuable receptor target for improving broad-spectrum planthopper resistance, potentially in combination with OsCslF6-based strategies
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Acts downstream of OsCslF6-mediated MLG biosynthesis; direct binding demonstrated only for OsLecRK1, not OsLecRK2

Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Contributes genetically to planthopper defence together with OsLecRK1
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Required together with OsLecRK1 for full OsCslF6-mediated resistance to BPH and WBPH
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. OsLecRK3 was knocked out together with OsLecRK1 in the F6OE4 background. 2. The double knockout abolished enhanced BPH and WBPH resistance conferred by OsCslF6 overexpression.
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: CRISPR double knockout; insect feeding assay
Common_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Double knockout seedlings were smaller than wild type under normal conditions
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential supporting target in receptor-mediated pest defence networks
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: No direct ligand-binding evidence for OsLecRK3 was presented in the provided text

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Synthesizes Poaceae-specific mixed-linkage β-1,3;1,4-D-glucan polymers in the cell wall from UDP-glucose-derived glucan units
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: leaf sheaths and stems; vascular bundle cell walls
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Reinforces vascular bundle cell wall thickness and provides MLG-derived oligosaccharides that activate defence signalling against brown planthopper and white-backed planthopper
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes MLG accumulation, thickens vascular bundle cell walls, and enhances resistance to BPH and WBPH; knockout reduces resistance and overexpression increases resistance
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. OsCslF6 was the major BPH- and JA-responsive CslF gene in leaf sheaths and stems. 2. CRISPR knockout reduced MLG content, vascular bundle size/cell wall thickness, and resistance to BPH/WBPH. 3. Overexpression increased MLG accumulation and enhanced resistance in growth room and field conditions. 4. OsCslF6 functions downstream of OsMYC2 and upstream of OsLecRK1-mediated defence signalling.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: CRISPR knockout; overexpression; metabolite quantification of MLG; insect feeding assays; TEM/confocal microscopy; genetic rescue with OsMYC2-RNAi background
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Mainly expressed in leaf sheaths and stems; induced by BPH infestation and MeJA treatment
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Leaf sheaths and stems during BPH feeding and after MeJA treatment
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Brown planthopper infestation; white-backed planthopper infestation; methyl jasmonate treatment; field and growth room conditions
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Promoters: Overexpression promoter not specified in provided text
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: key control point for MLG accumulation in BPH-responsive tissues
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Knockout caused dwarf phenotype; overexpression caused stronger morphological changes than wild type
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Strong candidate for engineering broad-spectrum planthopper resistance in rice and other cereals by increasing MLG deposition; promoter allele selection and gene overexpression/editing are promising strategies
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Promoter natural variation at SNP8/9 affects transcriptional activation and BPH resistance; overexpression rescues OsMYC2-RNAi susceptibility
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: Widely targeted metabolomics (Dataset S1); untargeted metabolomics (Dataset S2)

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Oxygenates polyunsaturated fatty acids in the JA biosynthetic pathway, contributing to JA and JA-Ile production
Pathway_Genes.Primary_Product: jasmonates including JA and JA-Ile precursors
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Hormonal activation of defence responses against BPH
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes JA accumulation and contributes to BPH resistance; knockout reduces wound-induced JA/JA-Ile and increases BPH susceptibility
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. OsLOX2 was induced by BPH infestation. 2. CRISPR mutants showed reduced wound-induced JA and JA-Ile. 3. oslox2 mutants were hypersensitive to BPH, and MeJA could partially rescue susceptibility.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: CRISPR knockout; hormone quantification; RT-qPCR; insect feeding assays; MeJA complementation
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Upregulated at 6, 12 and 24 h after BPH infestation
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Leaf sheaths and stems upon BPH feeding
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: No obvious morphological difference at 4 weeks reported
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Supports manipulating JA biosynthesis to enhance pest resistance, though broad hormonal effects may require caution
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Included because direct mutant analysis linked JA deficiency to reduced MLG accumulation and BPH resistance
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: Widely targeted metabolomics (Dataset S1); untargeted metabolomics (Dataset S2)

Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: MLG biosynthesis downstream of jasmonate signalling
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Activates OsCslF6 transcription by binding G-box motifs in its promoter
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: leaf sheaths and stems; vascular bundles
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Enhances cell wall thickness and defence signalling against planthoppers
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes MLG accumulation and BPH resistance through activation of OsCslF6
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. OsMYC2 knockdown lowered OsCslF6 transcript levels and MLG contents, whereas overexpression increased both. 2. ChIP-qPCR, EMSA, and dual-luciferase assays demonstrated direct binding and activation of the OsCslF6 promoter. 3. OsCslF6 overexpression rescued the BPH-sensitive phenotype of OsMYC2-RNAi plants.
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: RNAi; overexpression; RT-qPCR; MLG quantification; ChIP-qPCR; EMSA; dual-luciferase assay; genetic rescue by crossing
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Functionally responsive during BPH infestation; direct expression pattern not fully detailed in provided text
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Studied in BPH-treated seedlings and transgenic lines
Regulation_Genes.Upstream_Signals_or_Inputs: Jasmonate signalling activated by BPH feeding
Regulation_Genes.Applied_Promoters: Overexpression/RNAi promoters not specified in provided text
Regulation_Genes.Interacting_Proteins: OsCslF6 promoter G-box regions; OsJAZ proteins inferred from JA pathway model
Regulation_Genes.Protein_Complex_Involvement: Functions in JA signalling module with SCF^OsCOI1 and OsJAZ according to model
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Master JA-signalling regulator directly linking jasmonate perception to inducible MLG biosynthesis
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful regulatory target to boost inducible cell-wall based pest resistance, though broader JA-regulated pleiotropy should be considered
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Association of promoter AT-rich motif near G-box enhances OsMYC2-mediated activation of OsCslF6

Regulation_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: F-box protein; SCF ubiquitin ligase component
Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: Jasmonate signalling controlling MLG accumulation and defence responses
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Positively supports JA-responsive activation of OsMYC2-dependent genes including OsCslF6
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Supports defence against BPH via JA-dependent induction of MLG accumulation
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Required for full JA-mediated BPH resistance and BPH-induced MLG accumulation
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. OsCOI1a was induced by BPH infestation. 2. oscoi1a oscoi1b double mutants were less sensitive to MeJA and hypersensitive to BPH. 3. Mutants showed reduced BPH-induced MLG accumulation and reduced OsCslF6 induction.
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: CRISPR knockout in double-mutant combination; MeJA root inhibition assay; insect feeding assay; MLG quantification; RT-qPCR
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Upregulated at 6, 12 and 24 h after BPH infestation
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Leaf sheaths and stems upon BPH infestation
Regulation_Genes.Interacting_Proteins: OsCOI1b; OsJAZ proteins inferred from model
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Essential upstream JA receptor component enabling inducible MLG biosynthesis under herbivory
Regulation_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Residual MeJA sensitivity in double mutants suggests weak alleles or redundancy with OsCOI2
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Highlights importance of intact JA receptor signalling for pest resistance, though global JA signalling manipulation may have pleiotropic effects
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Experimentally assessed together with OsCOI1b as a double mutant
Regulation_Genes.Omics_Data: Widely targeted metabolomics (Dataset S1); untargeted metabolomics (Dataset S2)
